commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

repository.name: HaiClaude
repository.description: Native Haiku GUI launcher for Claude Code

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(gateway): allow OTEL trace export without authentication (#473)

The browser OTEL SDK sends trace data via POST to /otel/v1/traces.
The PublicPathMatcher only allowed GET/HEAD for public paths, causing
the POST to be rejected with 401 Unauthorized.

Add a new `unauthenticated-paths` config property for paths that
bypass authentication for ALL HTTP methods, distinct from `public-paths`
which only allow GET/HEAD for UI bootstrap endpoints. Move /otel from
public-paths to unauthenticated-paths.

Co-authored-by: Craig Klinker <craigklinker@velocityglobal.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(admin+security): harden Firestore rules and overhaul Admin user management

Security:
- firestore.rules: protect isPremium, hasUnlimitedCredits, and role fields from
  client-side tampering (only aiCreditsBalance+tier were blocked before — a user
  could write isPremium:true directly and bypass all credit checks)

Admin panel (SystemAdminView):
- Full subscription edit form per user: credits input, +10/+50/+100/+500/Reset
  buttons, Tier selector (Free/Pro/Unlimited), Premium checkbox, Unlimited checkbox,
  quick preset buttons (Free+50 / →Pro / →Unlimited)
- isPremium and hasUnlimitedCredits are now actually editable (were always passed
  as existing values before — impossible to change from UI)
- Subscription summary pills: Free/Pro/Unlimited counts + total credits at a glance
- Search/filter users by name, email, or UID
- Remove annoying window.confirm() — replaced by inline save/cancel buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use MySQL socket as reliable site running check (#16)

siteProvisioner.getSiteStatus() throws in the add-on context so the
check was never working. Replace with a check for the MySQL socket
file (~/.../Local/run/<siteId>/mysql/mysqld.sock) which is present
only when the site is running — the same check WP-CLI uses internally.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refine execution error terminology for precision and clarity

Updated coverage and framework descriptions to use more precise language:

Coverage Section:
- Financial Indemnity: "Direct monetary losses caused by incorrect or unauthorized execution"
- Authority Breaches → Execution Deviations: "Losses resulting from actions that deviate from defined transaction parameters"
- User Errors: "Losses caused by human misconfiguration, incorrect prompts, or improperly defined authority limits"
- AI Vendor Failures: "Losses caused by third-party infrastructure failures, API outages, or AI vendor service disruptions"
- Shadow AI: Added "Losses from" prefix for consistency

Framework Section:
- Flag: "Detect deviations from defined authority limits or execution parameters"
- Indemnify: "Reimburse for incorrect or unauthorized executions"

Alignment Section:
- Fast-Track Legal Review: "Pre-solve the execution risk question for enterprise risk teams"
- Operational Backstop: "Protect against AI Agent execution error or actions beyond authority limits"

Coverage Vacuum:
- Changed "execution error risk" to "execution risk" for brevity

All changes maintain consistent loss-based framing and precise terminology

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: replace invalid seed UUIDs with valid v4 UUIDs (#97)

* fix: replace invalid seed UUIDs with valid v4 UUIDs

The original seed used UUIDs like a0000000-... with version digit '0',
which fail Zod v4's stricter UUID validation (requires version 1-8).
Replaces all seed UUIDs with proper v4 UUIDs and adds migration 017
to update existing DB rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add seed UUID validation against Zod's UUID regex

Prevents future seed SQL files from using UUIDs that fail Zod v4
validation (requires version digit 1-8). Scans INSERT statements
in migration files 016+ and validates all hardcoded UUIDs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(digest): add daily market digest with top 5 deals by $/TH

- New daily digest sends top 5 cheapest listings (by $/TH) via Telegram
- Runs daily at 9 AM CT via GitHub Actions (daily_digest.yml)
- Reuses existing API + quality gates, no discount threshold
- Fix: hashprice unit conversion (API returns $/TH/day, code expects $/PH/day)
- Fix: _discount_pct() falsy check for 0.0 benchmark values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

at 2xl: (1536px+) so laptop screens are unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add EMP validation suite: 13 experiments proving paper claims

Validates all mathematical claims in the EMP paper and Innovations Register
through computational proof. Key results:

- DPI ceiling (Prop 7A): 0/10 violations across trials
- Capability Ratchet gate: rho=0.95 correctly accepts/rejects pruning
- Cardinality Barrier (I-03): proven necessary AND sufficient (exhaustive)
- ECF projection: 0.9996 retention, well above 0.904 floor
- MINE reliable to dim~20; SMILE 16.2% tighter at dim=50
- CoW memory: 131x savings on real Switch-Base-8 model
- Tikhonov damping: eps=1e-4 gives kappa=2440 (stable)

New innovations (Experiment 13):
- Adaptive rho: rho(f) = 1 - 0.49*f^0.90 replaces fixed rho=0.95
- MINE dimensional correction factor: c(d) = 1 + 0.37*log(d/2)
- Probabilistic compounding bound: 20% tighter than worst-case

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Linear-style Display panel for notes table (#29)

* feat: alerts system — SRS due, snooze expired, stale notes

Adds a computed alerts feed with 3 initial alert types:
- SRS due: permanent notes with overdue spaced repetition
- Snooze expired: inbox notes past their snooze reviewAt
- Stale notes: capture notes untouched for 7+ days

Alerts are derived from state (not stored), only dismissed IDs
are persisted. Includes /alerts page, sidebar nav with count badge,
store slice, and v19 migration.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix logo: correct proxy matcher + restore original logo.png

Root cause: the proxy's complex regex was unreliable on Vercel's edge runtime,
causing it to intercept requests for /logo.png and redirect unauthenticated
requests (like the login page) away from the image.

Fix: replace the regex matcher with an explicit list of protected routes.
Static files in public/ are never touched by the proxy.
Also remove BullLogo SVG and restore the original logo.png image.

commit.message:
fix: scan page now uses official allergen arrays for preloaded restaurants

ALL_MENUS was stripping item.allergens when mapping MOCK_RESTAURANTS to
string arrays, so text detection alone ran on item names like "Big Mac"
(finds nothing) and the verified-dataset source caused false Likely Safe
results. EnrichedMenuSource now carries fullItems, and the results memo
merges official allergens into detected before scoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deploy: replace VERCEL_URL with live URL

https://roof-deal-pipeline.vercel.app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace activeprobe with smartfuzz provider

SmartFuzz fixes three critical issues with activeprobe:
1. Tech detection dependency — universal probes now hit every host
   regardless of detected tech (finds Spring Boot behind CDN/proxy)
2. Single path, zero variation — prefix expansion tries 11 path
   prefixes (/manage/, /admin/, /api/, /v1/, /v2/ etc.)
3. Limited probe lists — expanded from ~40 to ~120 probes across
   Spring Boot, Swagger, phpMyAdmin, WordPress, Go pprof, etc.

New features:
- Runtime tech discovery: /actuator/health 200 → triggers all Spring probes
- Discovery-based fuzzing from endpoints.json (sibling paths, ext swap)
- CDN-aware target classification (skip/critical_only/full modes)
- CORS misconfiguration checking
- Configurable max_probes_per_host limit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix chart dropdowns prepopulation and map loading

- Fixed chart filter dropdowns (Brand, Model, Serial) to prepopulate from database on page load
- Moved populateChartFilters() call to execute right after loadAllReports() completes
- Fixed Google Maps API endpoint from /api/config/google-maps-key to /api/google-maps-key

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add error handling and graceful degradation (P10-2)

Backend:
- Orchestrator tracks per-agent errors and includes them in composed
  surface payloads so the frontend can display partial-failure info
- Structured error logging with traceId throughout the pipeline
- Agent crashes are isolated via Promise.allSettled; partial results
  from completed agents are still emitted

Frontend:
- ErrorSurface component shows user-friendly error messages (maps
  agent IDs to readable names, sanitizes raw error strings)
- StaleIndicator badge on surfaces when data may be incomplete
- Connection banner shows when WebSocket disconnects, auto-hides
  on reconnect
- HomePage handles "error" message type from server

Types:
- AgentError interface added to ComposedLayout for structured
  error propagation from backend to frontend

Closes #46

that doesn't account for compilation time.
